name: Pelias Open-Source Geocoder
what_it_does: Open-source modular geocoder with REST API — enabling developers to self-host a full geocoding stack using OpenStreetMap, OpenAddresses, Who's on First, and Natural Earth data, with forward/reverse geocoding, autocomplete, and structured address search, used internally by companies like Apple and Snapchat and available as a hosted service via Geocode.earth.
use_cases: Self-hosting a full geocoding stack via Pelias for privacy-preserving address search
Forward and reverse geocoding via Pelias API for location-based application features
Address autocomplete via Pelias for user-facing search interfaces
Running Pelias on own infrastructure to avoid geocoding API costs at scale
Using Geocode.earth managed Pelias for production geocoding without self-hosting burden
not_for: Teams without DevOps resources for self-hosted Elasticsearch and data pipeline management
Real-time traffic or navigation data (Pelias is geocoding only, not routing)
Teams needing a managed zero-setup geocoding API (use Nominatim API or Geoapify instead)
best_when: A company needs a fully self-hosted, privacy-preserving geocoder at scale — where Pelias provides complete control over data, privacy, and infrastructure without per-call licensing fees, or when using Geocode.earth for managed Pelias hosting.
avoid_when: You need a simple managed geocoding API without infrastructure to maintain, or need routing, navigation, or real-time traffic data.

auth.notes: Self-hosted: no auth required (you control the server). Geocode.earth managed service: API key via api_key query parameter. No OAuth2. No scope granularity. Self-hosted deployments can add auth at reverse proxy layer. Open source MIT license — no vendor lock-in. Elasticsearch backend required for self-hosted.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Self-hosted: completely free (infrastructure costs only). Geocode.earth: free trial available
pricing.paid_tiers: Geocode.earth from $49/month (managed hosting). Self-hosted: infrastructure costs only
pricing.notes: Created by Mapzen (NYC, shutdown 2018). Rescued by Linux Foundation as open project. Geocode.earth is the primary commercial managed hosting provider (run by original Mapzen engineers). MIT license. Node.js microservices architecture. Elasticsearch backend. Data sources: OpenStreetMap (Valhalla), OpenAddresses, Who's on First, Natural Earth, TIGER. Used by: Apple (internally), Snapchat, NYC government. Docker Compose for self-hosted setup. Modular — enable only data sources you need. Active GitHub community. No vendor lock-in — same API whether self-hosted or Geocode.earth.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: no limits. Geocode.earth: varies by plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with simple API key (Geocode.earth) or no auth (self-hosted). MIT license. Elasticsearch backend. No webhooks. User-controlled data residency. Self-hosted option for unlimited volume. Geocode.earth for managed hosting. Node.js microservices architecture.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All geocoding requests fully idempotent. Same address always returns same result. Pure read API — no state mutations.
known_agent_gotchas: Self-hosted requires Elasticsearch cluster — significant infrastructure overhead
Data import pipeline takes hours for full OpenStreetMap dataset
Self-hosted API path: /v1/search, /v1/reverse, /v1/autocomplete — identical to Geocode.earth
Pelias has no rate limits on self-hosted — unlimited volume for agents
Geocode.earth managed service uses same API interface — can switch without code changes
Address quality depends on OpenAddresses data coverage — varies by country

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS depends on deployment config (self-hosted: user responsibility). User-controlled data residency (self-hosted). MIT open source — fully auditable. No vendor tracking of queries (self-hosted). Geocode.earth: TLS enforced, API key auth. Self-hosted: add auth at reverse proxy layer. Location data (addresses) may be sensitive PII — self-hosted keeps data local.
